title: Chetan didn't pay, Ketan didn't return: Guard recalls moments before man's murder at Pune fort
content: A security guard's account at Lohagad Fort has shed light on the chilling events leading to Ketan Agarwal's death. He observed Ketan arriving with Siya Goyal, followed by Chetan Chaudhary, who appeared to deliberately conceal his identity. Police allege Siya and Chetan, her lover and co-conspirator, then pushed Ketan into a gorge, with Siya later fabricating a story of an accidental fall. The couple is now in custody.

title: Why Xi Jinping’s anti-corruption crusade is now policing loyalty, lifestyles and even luck
content: China's decade-long anti-corruption drive under Xi Jinping has transformed from tackling graft to enforcing political loyalty and ideological conformity. Party watchdogs now scrutinize officials' beliefs, associations, and leisure activities, extending beyond traditional bribery to include "political discipline" and "elegant corruption." This broadens the campaign's scope to ensure strict adherence to Beijing's directives and Xi's policy priorities across the Communist Party apparatus.

title: BJP claims IAF tender for 36 Rafales exposes Pak propaganda, says 'Rahul owes explanation'
content: The BJP has strongly criticized Rahul Gandhi, accusing him of echoing Pakistan's propaganda regarding the Rafale jets during Operation Sindoor. The party highlighted a recent IAF tender for support for all 36 Rafale jets as proof that Pakistan's claims of shooting them down were false. BJP leaders argue Gandhi questioned the Indian government instead of demanding evidence from Islamabad, thereby legitimizing an adversary's disinformation campaign.

title: Sooryavanshi to get a separate changing room in England: ECB reveals why
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, recently called up to India’s national team, could become the country’s youngest international cricketer if he debuts against Ireland. During the England tour, he will use a separate changing room under ICC and ECB safeguarding rules for under-16 players. The ECB and BCCI are coordinating arrangements, while his parents are expected to accompany him throughout the tour.

title: Israel's actions against Gaza children amount to genocidal acts, UN says
content: A UN commission report alleges Israeli forces are committing genocide in Gaza, citing the deliberate targeting of children and "unprecedented death, injury and trauma." The report highlights over 250 child fatalities since October and a systematic pattern of strikes on healthcare facilities. Israel has vehemently rejected these findings, calling the report a "propaganda piece" and a "political blood libel."

title: America's 89-year-old company Polaroid is 'warning' Americans about datacenters
content: Polaroid's new campaign is raising alarms about the significant water consumption of AI data centers.  Highlighting concerns over the environmental impact, the vintage camera company urges people to enjoy natural water resources before they are depleted. While not anti-digital, Polaroid champions human connection and the preservation of natural elements, sparking a wider debate on AI's resource demands.

title: Ketan Agarwal case: When women become accused in the dark world of relationship crimes
content: Disturbing crime cases in India reveal how relationships built on trust can allegedly turn into deadly betrayals. Investigations into incidents like the Lohagad Fort murder and the Meghalaya honeymoon killing point towards secret relationships and conspiracies. Victims were reportedly targeted by those they knew and trusted, raising questions about the darker side of personal disputes.

title: Mumbai local train murder: Airport worker nabbed; how argument over coach door led to stabbing
content: A Mumbai airport worker, Roshan Suvarna, was apprehended in Panvel following a fatal stabbing on a local train. The incident occurred Tuesday night between Andheri and Borivali after an argument with passenger Mayank Lohar over closing a coach door during heavy rain. Lohar sustained a fatal abdominal wound and later died at Shatabdi Hospital. Police identified Suvarna through extensive CCTV analysis.

title: Watch: How Argentine talisman Lionel Messi celebrated his 39th birthday
content: Lionel Messi, celebrating his 39th birthday, defied expectations by hitting the gym for an intense workout instead of taking a break. The football icon's dedication was evident in a viral Instagram video showcasing his commitment to peak performance.  Messi's continued brilliance is central to Argentina's success in the FIFA World Cup 2026, where he has already broken scoring records.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM swaps 1,000 workers with 50 robots
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: 'Putin already compromised': Lavrov says Russia won't bend again on Ukraine, cites Trump talks
content: According to Lavrov, Putin agreed to proposals put forward by the United States during the Alaska meeting and reached an understanding with Trump on how the fighting in Ukraine could be brought to an end and how broader discussions could proceed afterwards. Lavrov said Russia remained committed to those agreements and would not support alternative interim arrangements or bend to ultimatums.

title: Kolkata warehouse collapse: 3 dead, many still trapped; top developments
content: A frantic rescue operation is underway in West Kolkata after an under-construction warehouse collapsed, trapping over 20 workers. Cranes, bulldozers, and specialized teams are working tirelessly to free those buried under the debris. Eleven injured have been rescued and hospitalized. Authorities are deploying advanced equipment, including sniffer dogs and drones, to locate survivors amidst the wreckage.

title: Selection row and disciplinary issues: Pakistan's T20 WC campaign in crisis
content: Pakistan's Women's T20 World Cup campaign is marred by internal strife, with reports of player-management friction and selection disputes. Following a string of losses, captain Fatima Sana admitted the team played its 'worst cricket.'  Allegations include disagreements over player selection and disciplinary issues, casting a shadow over their performance despite positive pre-tournament preparations.

title: After Anthropic curbs, Canadian CEO backs cutting foreign AI ties
content: Canadian AI chief Aidan Gomez warns democracies against 'renting' AI, calling it a national security risk. He highlights the danger of relying on foreign tech giants, citing recent US restrictions as a wake-up call. Gomez advocates for "Sovereign AI," urging nations to control their AI supply chains. He suggests international alliances are key to achieving this, rather than individual countries attempting full independence due to immense costs.

title: Sir Alex Ferguson's favourite general robs England of its roar
content: On Day 13, Carlos Queiroz's Ghana poured cold water on England's optimism, Ronaldo broke his duck against minnows Uzbekistan and Croatia turned back the clock. Read this and more in today's Offside.

title: Taylor Swift is reportedly struggling with the Blake Lively fallout
content: Taylor Swift and Blake Lively’s bond has only worsened over the last few months as rumors swirl about the actress being invited to the pop star’s wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen in the next few weeks. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce focus on their wedding, it seems the pop star has gone through a wave of emotions.

title: Ronaldo vs Messi at FIFA World Cup 2026: CR7 says Portugal vs Argentina clash would be 'top''
content: Cristiano Ronaldo ended a difficult week with two goals as Portugal beat Uzbekistan 5-0, topping Group K at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old became the first player to score in six men's World Cups. After the match, Ronaldo called a potential showdown with Lionel Messi "awesome," a clash that could materialise in the quarterfinals if Portugal secure top spot in their group against Colombia on Saturday.

title: NHL Trade rumors: Maple Leafs target 2-time Stanley Cup champion goalie
content: NHL insider Elliotte Friedman has identified veteran goaltender Sergei Bobrovsky as a realistic target for the Toronto Maple Leafs, who are actively exploring the goaltending market after moving Joseph Woll. Bobrovsky, set to become an unrestricted free agent after his contract with the Florida Panthers expires, gives Toronto a financially efficient route to upgrade in net without surrendering trade assets. Two existing Maple Leafs, Anthony Stolarz and Steve Lorentz, both share strong personal bonds with Bobrovsky from his time in Florida, adding an unusual layer of familiarity to an already compelling fit.

title: Sooryavanshi's reaction to brother Ashirvad's another century goes viral
content: India youngster Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's reaction to younger brother Ashirvad's latest century has gone viral on social media. Vaibhav shared an Instagram story after the 10-year-old smashed 168 off 119 balls, including 19 fours and six sixes. Already making waves in Bihar cricket, Ashirvad recently scored 103 off 87 in a practice match, drawing praise from fans and family alike.

title: Sooryavanshi mania triggers ticket rush in Ireland ahead of possible India debut
content: Excitement is building in Ireland for 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential India debut in the upcoming T20I series. His IPL exploits have generated significant buzz, with fans from across Europe eager to witness the event. Former cricketer MV Narasimha Rao believes Sooryavanshi's style resembles Gary Sobers and advises the young talent to stay grounded amidst the growing hype.

title: R Madhavan flaunts Rs 40 lakh limited-edition rose gold watch at Padma Shri ceremony
content: Actor R Madhavan received the prestigious Padma Shri from President Droupadi Murmu, a moment shared with his family. His exquisite 18-karat rose gold Jalsa Nebula watch, featuring a miniature painting inspired by Jaipur's Hawa Mahal and valued at Rs 40 lakhs, stole the spotlight. Madhavan expressed profound gratitude for the honour, dedicating it to his supporters and the world of cinema.

title: Elon Musk loses trillionaire status: Here's why his net worth fell
content: Elon Musk, once the world's first trillionaire, has seen his net worth dip below the 13-figure mark to $957 billion. A significant sell-off in Tesla and SpaceX shares, following SpaceX's IPO surge, has led to this decline. Concerns over an AI market bubble and potential interest rate hikes are contributing to the downturn in tech stocks.

title: Identify, repair, recast: 44 flyovers over 15 years old to undergo structural audit
content: Delhi's Public Works Department is launching structural audits for 44 flyovers older than 15 years, with some dating back three decades. This proactive measure, costing Rs 11 crore, aims to identify and address potential safety risks before they become critical. The initiative underscores a commitment to preventive maintenance and ensuring the long-term safety and reliability of the city's vital infrastructure for its citizens.

title: Lohagad Fort murder in Pune: Ketan Agarwal's father says they initially rejected marriage proposal
content: Tragedy struck a Pune family after their son, Ketan Agarwal, was allegedly murdered by his fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her alleged lover. Police claim the couple conspired to push Ketan from Lohagad Fort, staging it as an accident.  Ketan's parents, who had known Goyal's family for decades and trusted her implicitly, expressed shock and devastation at the betrayal, questioning why their son had to die if the marriage was unwanted.

title: Builder kept homebuyer's money for 10 years, court orders to refund Rs 5.25 lakh
content: A Hoshiarpur consumer commission has ordered a builder and housing society to refund Rs 5.25 lakh to a software engineer. The project, "Krisha Heights," promised completion within four years of a 2015 application, but remained on paper for over a decade. The commission slammed the delay as an unfair trade practice, stating builders cannot collect funds without approvals and use them as interest-free capital. The refund includes 7% annual interest.

title: Ronaldo matches Messi in rare FIFA World Cup record with brace against Uzbekistan
content: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Portugal beat Uzbekistan 5-0 to secure their first win of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old became the first player to score in six different World Cups and Portugal’s all-time leading World Cup scorer with 10 goals. Ronaldo also matched Lionel Messi and Michael Laudrup as both his country's youngest and oldest World Cup scorer.

title: Why did stock market rally today? BSE Sensex closes around 800 points up
content: A recovery in South Korean markets and supportive global developments also aided the rally. The Sensex climbed almost 791 points to settle at 76,991, while the Nifty 50 gained about 198 points to close at 24,022.

title: Matt Henry joins Jasprit Bumrah, becomes first Kiwi in 36 years to ...
content: Matt Henry has achieved a historic feat, joining Jasprit Bumrah at the pinnacle of the ICC Test bowling rankings. Both pacers now share the World No. 1 spot with 870 rating points. Henry's remarkable 11-wicket haul at The Oval propelled him to this elite status, ending a 36-year wait for a New Zealand seamer to reach the top.

title: ‘Constructive, forward-looking’: India, US conclude ministerial-level talks on FTA
content: India and the US concluded ministerial talks on a proposed trade deal, aiming for an interim pact before a US tariff deadline next month. Discussions focused on market access, digital trade, and reducing barriers. This follows recent high-level meetings and aims to deepen economic ties, with both nations reviewing progress on key trade elements and potential tariff adjustments.

title: INS Udaygiri, INS Kavaratti dock in Vietnam as India expands Indo-Pacific naval presence
content: Indian Navy warships INS Udaygiri and INS Kavaratti have arrived in Vietnam, marking a significant step in deepening defense ties. This deployment highlights India's growing naval presence and its commitment to maritime security in Southeast Asia. The visit underscores the strong strategic partnership between India and Vietnam, emphasizing shared concerns for a stable, rules-based maritime order in the Indo-Pacific.

title: Captain Shubman Gill overtakes Virat Kohli in latest ICC ODI rankings
content: In a remarkable turn of events, Shubman Gill now ranks second in the ICC ODI batting rankings, overtaking fellow Indian star Virat Kohli, who drops to third. Gill showcased his exceptional skills versus Afghanistan, scoring a century and claiming Player of the Series honors. Rohit Sharma follows closely at fourth place. Kohli's injury has created this opportunity for Gill, as they gear up to face England soon.

title: After 15 deaths, Lucknow authorities to demolish residential building-turned-commercial hub
content: Following a tragic fire that claimed 15 lives, the Lucknow Development Authority has posted a demolition notice on the Aliganj building. The structure, approved for residential use, was allegedly operating commercially. An inquiry is underway into officials' roles regarding past violations and the revocation of a previous demolition order. Authorities are also investigating broader lapses that allowed the establishment to function.

title: 3 dead, 11 injured after warehouse shed collapses in Kolkata; rescue ops under way
content: A massive rescue operation is underway in Kolkata's Taratala area after an under-construction warehouse collapsed, trapping several people. Emergency services, including NDRF and army personnel, are on-site, using heavy machinery to clear debris and search for survivors. Four individuals have been rescued so far. The state government has established control rooms and released helpline numbers for the incident.

title: Portugal's FIFA World Cup R32 qualification scenario explained
content: Colombia booked their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 after defeating DR Congo 1-0 in their Group K clash on Wednesday. The South American side made it two wins from two matches after previously beating Uzbekistan 3-1. With six points from their opening two games, Colombia have secured qualification for the knockout stage with one group match still to play.

title: Will Mumbai schools and colleges remain closed tomorrow amid IMD orange alert?
content: Mumbai grappled with heavy rainfall and severe waterlogging on June 23, causing significant traffic disruptions. The IMD has issued an orange alert for the city, warning of intense showers and thunderstorms. Despite the adverse weather, no official announcement has been made regarding school or college closures for tomorrow. Educational institutions are expected to operate normally unless further directives are issued by the administration.

title: Pune murder case: Why not elope? Siya's lover reveals motive behind 'plot to kill' Ketan
content: A planned murder disguised as a trekking accident has been uncovered near Pune. Siya Goyal and her friend Chetan Chaudhary allegedly conspired to kill Ketan Agarwal, Siya's fiancé. Police suspect Siya feared family shame more than eloping with Chetan. Suspicious CCTV footage of a hooded figure at Lohagad Fort led investigators to the couple, who are now in custody.

title: As the monsoon cools India, a wave of gut infections sweeps the country
content: Monsoon's arrival in India brings relief from heat but also a surge in stomach ailments. High humidity and standing water create a breeding ground for bacteria, leading to widespread food and waterborne illnesses. Experts warn about contaminated street food and water, with vulnerable groups like children and the elderly at higher risk. Practicing strict hygiene, boiling water, and avoiding risky foods are crucial to prevent these seasonal infections.

title: MahaRERA portal changed: How buyers can check projects and complaints now
content: Maharashtra's homebuyers now have access to MahaCRITI, the new online hub replacing the previous portal for MahaRERA services. With this digital transformation, users can easily find project details, developer information, and submit complaints. This initiative is designed to enhance efficiency, enabling buyers to confirm project registrations and monitor their concerns. Any pending applications from the old system have been closed but may be refiled through the new platform.

title: 'They use religion to defame me': Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann rejects 'sacrilege' allegations
content: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann has vehemently denied allegations of "sacrilegious conduct," calling them a "dirty politics" campaign by rivals. He stated that a viral video controversy, linked to an alleged Rs 10 lakh deal for a forensic report, is being exploited to tarnish his image. Mann insists the person in the video is an actor, not him, and accuses opposition parties of using religion for political gain.

title: Airfares could rise by up to 25% as jet fuel costs surge, says McKinsey
content: Global jet fuel supplies are facing significant pressure due to geopolitical events and refinery limitations, according to McKinsey. This is driving up costs for airlines, with potential airfare hikes of 20-25% expected as fuel prices surge. Inventories remain low, and key exporting regions are restricting shipments, creating a challenging outlook for the summer travel season.

title: Mumbai local train horror: 22-year-old stabbed to death after row over shutting door
content: A tragic incident unfolded on a Mumbai local train where a 22-year-old man was fatally stabbed after an argument over a coach door during heavy rain. The dispute turned violent between Andheri and Borivali, leading to the victim's death. The accused fled the scene at Borivali station. Authorities are actively searching for the suspect, reviewing CCTV footage, and questioning witnesses.

title: UK heatwave puts dogs at risk: Breeds most vulnerable to heatstroke
content: The soaring temperatures in the UK are increasingly dangerous for dogs, especially for breeds like French Bulldogs, English Bulldogs, Pugs, Chow Chows, and Saint Bernards. Their unique physical traits make it challenging for them to manage heat, raising the risk of heatstroke. Dog owners should ensure their pets stay cool indoors, have plenty of water, and avoid outdoor activities during the hottest parts of the day to prevent health hazards.

title: Oath Invalid, Kerala Court Orders Fresh Swearing-In For 20 BJP Councillors
content: The petitioner had argued that elected representatives are required to take the oath either in the name of God or in the name of the Constitution, and that invoking specific deities was contrary to the prescribed oath and constitutional principles.

title: Indian Man Jailed In Singapore For 6 Months Over Harassing Air Hostess
content: 35-year-old Akash Tiwari followed the flight attendant into the galley and stood extremely close to her, after which she told him to stay away. He kept following her even though she left, which prompted her to issue a warning to him.

title: "He Was 21, Had Entire Life Ahead Of Him": Family In Tears After Mumbai Local Murder
content: Mayank Lohar was travelling in the first-class compartment of a Churchgate-Nallasopara fast local on Tuesday night when he was murdered.

title: Air India Delhi-Amritsar Flight Briefly Enters Pak Airspace, Probe On
content: The incident has been reported to the regulatory authorities, the airline said, adding that the matter is being investigated.

title: Non-Tariff Barriers, Market Access: India, US Review Core Trade Pact Issues
content: The discussions focused on pathways to conclude an interim agreement as an important milestone towards a comprehensive BTA, the commerce ministry said.

title: Man Visits Married Lover At Night. Her 2nd Boyfriend Severs His Hand
content: Arun was initially treated at a local hospital before being shifted to a hospital in Mangaluru for advanced treatment. His condition is critical.

title: Cop Stops Commuters Just On Time, Prevents Tragedy During Sikkim Landslide
content: Suk Bahadur Tamang had just completed his night duty and was on his way home when he noticed rocks beginning to fall from the hillside at around 9.30 am.

title: Man Stabbed To Death In Moving Mumbai Local Train After Row Over Closing Door
content: The victim, identified as Mayank Lohar, was travelling in the first-class compartment of a Churchgate-Nallasopara fast local when he got into a heated argument with another passenger between Goregaon and Kandivali.

title: Eggs Out Of Midday Meal Scheme In Bengal. Trinamool Says "Told You So"
content: While ISKCON is expected to provide good food, it would be entirely vegetarian and that has raised the hackles of Bengalis - barring a few exceptions, all proud non-vegetarians.

title: Nuclear Weapons 'Only' Guarantee Against Global War: Russia
content: The last nuclear arms control treaty between Russia and the United States, New START, expired in February, releasing restrictions on the world's two largest nuclear powers.

title: Kolkata Warehouse Roof Collapse Updates: 3 Dead, Rescue Operations Underway
content: Kolkata Warehouse Collapse Updates: Several workers were engaged in construction work at the warehouse when the roof of the three-floor building collapsed.

title: 3 Killed, 40 Feared Trapped As Warehouse Roof Collapses In Kolkata
content: Several workers were engaged in construction work at the warehouse when the roof of the three-floor building collapsed.

title: Shocking UN Report Reveals AI Is Learning Sexism, Racism From Humans
content: A UN study of 133 AI systems found many reinforced sexist and racial stereotypes, with some portraying women as sexual objects, property or subordinate to men.

title: 23 Pilgrims Injured As Minibus Plunges Into Gorge In J&K's Kathua
content: Officials said the pilgrims were injured after a minibus carrying devotees to the revered Sukrala Mata shrine near Billawar town of the district dropped into a gorge in the Simbli area when the driver lost control of the wheel.

title: Case Against DMK Supporter Over 'Controversial' Remark Against Vijay
content: The DMK has condemned the police action, accusing the TVK government of attempting to suppress the voices of its critics.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Indian Parties Are Beginning To Look Like Political 'Spare Parts'
content: India has thousands of political parties on paper. Some are genuine vehicles of aspiration. But others are beginning to look like political spare parts: useful when the main engine needs reassembly. Maharashtra and West Bengal are examples.

title: Air Force Document Punctures Pakistan's Claim Of Downing Rafales
content: The document shows planning is underway for the maintenance and support of all 36 Rafale fighter jets acquired from France.

title: India's Rising Nighttime Temperatures Should Worry Us More Than We Think
content: In the last decade (2012-2022), Mumbai saw 15 additional very warm nights per summer, Bengaluru (11), Bhopal and Jaipur (7 each), Delhi (6), and Chennai (4), as per CEEW report.

title: 'Deal To End War Is A Declaration Of US Defeat': Top Iranian Negotiator
content: "The Islamabad understanding was not the result of pressure and coercion, but rather the result of the resistance and authority of the brave Iranian nation," Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said.

title: Expert panel set up to formulate SOP to govern entry of zoo staff into animal enclosures, Central Zoo Authority tells Karnataka High Court
content: A petition in the High Court of Karnataka has listed 17 incidents in zoological parks across the country in the last decade, including seven animal attacks in Karnataka

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Naidu launches sale of gold from Jonnagiri project in Kurnool district
content: First consignment handed over to Andhra Pradesh jewellers as mine gears up to generate employment and strengthen the State’s mineral economy

title: They serve as they study, says Supreme Court, backing eased NEET-Super Speciality cut-offs for government doctors
content: Access for in-service government doctors to speciality medical courses must become easier, as their healing skills would not be used for personal profit but for the benefit of the larger public, the Bench says

title: Authorities intensify drive against coaching centres violating safety norms in Uttar Pradesh
content: A joint team of the Lucknow Development Authority (LDA), fire department and electricity department carried out inspections of coaching institutes in the Aliganj-Kapoorthala area, officials said

title: India, U.S. note ‘substantial progress’ on trade deal talks as U.S. Trade Representative Greer ends two-day visit to Delhi
content: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer held multiple meetings with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal; however, the two sides have not yet been able to announce a deadline by which the trade deals will be finalised

title: India-U.S. interim deal requires only ‘final touches’; Greer to visit New Delhi on June 23-24
content: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal says a deal will not be possible until it is clear India will face lower tariffs than its competitors

title: Congress urges Centre not to rush to sign trade pact with the U.S.
content: Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh says Prime Minister should not give in to pressure from the U.S. He says the U.S. is using its probe into alleged unfair trade practices to pressure New Delhi into formalising the proposed agreement

title: Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs to meet Lok Sabha Speaker today over merger of 6 leaders with Shinde camp
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) has questioned the lack of transparency surrounding the entry of six MPs into the ruling Shiv Sena led by Eknath Shinde; it had submitted a letter to the Speaker seeking a hearing on this.

title: Three dead after under-construction warehouse collapses at Taratala in Kolkata; rescue efforts on
content: “All the agencies are working together. We have rescued 21 persons of which three have died,” said Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari at the accident site, adding 18 are still trapped and contact has been established with them

title: Of Parai, Tholkaapiyam, and battling a stereotype
content: The Parai is at the forefront of discussions of caste empowerment. It has played an undeniable role in enabling the migration of the oppressed from the margins to the mainstream. But under that history is a fact: the Parai was, first, a language – and so, just as much, an identity

title: Mamata seeks Madan Mitra as chief whip after showcausing Firhad; Speaker says matter sub-judice
content: The move came hours after the TMC leadership show-caused Firhad Hakim, accusing him of anti-party activities for accepting a position in the rebel camp's parallel organisational structure announced on June 22

title: After Jonnagiri, Andhra Pradesh set to auction a promising gold block in Chittoor
content: The 273-hectare Chigurgunta-Bisanatham block, on the southern edge of the Kolar belt, holds an estimated 22.16 lakh tonnes of ore; bids are open until July 9

title: Maharashtra RTI rules amendments trigger criticism
content: New RTI Rules 2026 introduce higher fees, photo ID requirement and restrictions on applications; Anna Hazare threatens hunger strike

title: Kolkata under-construction warehouse collapse LIVE: Three killed, 18 injured in Taratala accident, says West Bengal CM
content: According to locals present at the accident site, 50 to 60 workers were working at the site when the accident took place

title: Congress to launch fierce protests over exam scams and corruption
content: Anti-youth policies, rampant corruption, and anti-democratic actions of the BJP regime have pushed the future of the nation’s youth and students into complete darkness, says senior Congress leader Kodikunnil Suresh

title: Fiscal prudence, the need of the hour for Tamil Nadu’s power utilities
content: The approach of successive governments to not hike user or service provider charges regularly has led to power sector entities accumulating debt

title: Jonnagiri: India’s largest private gold mine set to yield a tonne of gold a year
content: About 12 tonnes of gold lie under Jonnagiri, enough to yield some 400 kg in 2026-27 and pay Andhra Pradesh a 4% royalty

title: What constitutes a ‘workplace’ under POSH Act?
content: The High Court, in its judgment dated June 16, set aside the findings of an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) that had held a State Bank of India (SBI) employee guilty of sexual harassment based on an incident that occurred during such travel

title: Chose education portfolio to bring social change, says HRD Minister Nara Lokesh
content: The Minister inaugurated school buildings built for ₹1.4 crore under the P4 programme and said the focus must now move to academic results

title: Bombay High Court says shared auto not 'workplace' under PoSH Act, sets aside ICC order
content: The Court, in its June 22 order, set aside the findings of an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) that had held a State Bank of India (SBI) employee guilty of sexual harassment based on an incident that occurred during such travel

title: India eyes 100 GW as global hydropower shifts to ‘water battery’: report
content: The 2026 Global Hydropower Outlook says China continues to dominate global hydropower development, with more than 300 GW, including 218 GW of pumped storage, under construction

title: Get ready for Chennai’s Rainbow Pride March on June 28
content: In Chennai, a transformative coalition of queer individuals have come together for 18 years to celebrate identity, politics, and years of advocacy, to live as they are. Their annual pride march on June 28 proves that the rainbow flag will continue to flutter high despite impediments

title: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill and the question of identity: Who gets to define a trans life?
content: As India tightens its transgender law, love, desire, and dating apps reveal how trans people are still asked for proof before acceptance

title: Deaths could have been avoided in Lucknow blaze if authorities had enforced safety norms: Akhilesh
content: Claiming that emergency response systems had deteriorated, he said people trapped inside the building continued calling for help and questioned the adequacy of rescue equipment suggesting that more lives could have been saved through quicker intervention

title: Lucknow building blaze: Suspended fire officer withdraws allegation of 'lapses' levelled at senior
content: "I was misled into making the video. I am extremely saddened by the loss of 15 lives in the incident," says  suspended Fire Station Second Officer

title: SIT inspects Lucknow fire incident site; Congress alleges Uttar Pradesh government’s negligence responsible for tragedy
content: SIT members Amrit Abhijat, Additional Chief Secretary, Tourism and Culture Department, and Praveen Kumar, ADG, Lucknow Zone, conducted a detailed inspection of the entire building for over an hour in the morning; a team from the Uttar Pradesh Forensic Science Laboratory (FSL) also collected crucial evidence from the site

title: Congress yet to take a call on Kochi Corporation Deputy Mayor’s post
content: It has been more than a month since Deepak Joy, Thripunithura MLA,  resigned from the position of the Deputy Mayor as well as the councillor after his win in the Assembly polls

title: Rescued Siberian huskies still waiting for homes as legal delays stall adoption in Hyderabad
content: Nearly two months after their rescue, 11 Siberian huskies and a German shepherd remain in Gajularamaram shelter.  Chargesheet yet to be filed against accused.

title: BJP’s two-thirds majority bid aimed at scrapping reservation, alleges Congress
content: I think he decided to take revenge on that day for that humiliation he had been forced to suffer and now he is busy with the 'todh-phod ki rajniti', says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh

title: Anbumani calls on father and PMK founder Ramadoss
content: Dr. Anbumani, accompanied by his wife and Dharmapuri MLA Sowmiya Anbumani and other family members, visited his parents on the occasion of their 61st wedding anniversary

title: Two youth killed in bike accident near Nedumbassery in Ernakulam
content: Deceased identified as Gokul and Ashwin, both in their early 20s and natives of Irinjalakuda, Thrissur

title: KEA to seek Rural and Kannada medium certificates in digital form for CET, recruitment exams in Karnataka
content: Has submitted a request to the Department of School Education and Literacy (DSEL) to distribute these certificates to candidates in digital form

title: Jaishankar, South Korean FM discuss deepening strategic partnership in Seoul
content: The talks focused on follow-up measures in the key areas of trade, investment and finance, agreed during South Korean President Lee Jae-myung’s state visit to India in April

title: Watch: What is ammonia poisoning?
content: At least 9 people have died and around 69 others have been hospitalised after an ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing plant in the Tiruvallur district of Tamil Nadu. The incident has raised concerns about ammonia poisoning and the dangers associated with industrial gas leaks. So, what exactly is ammonia poisoning? What are the symptoms? How is it treated?

title: Shiv Sena at 60: Fragmentation of Maharashtra’s ‘Tiger’
content: From being a Thackeray-led party as the voice of the ‘Marathi Manoos’ in 1966, Shiv Sena is now reduced to playing second-fiddle to BJP and Thackeray’s control decimated

title: Emotional Uddhav offers to step down as Shiv Sena (UBT) president, Eknath Shinde hints at more defections
content: Mr. Thackeray calls for starting ‘Operation Lotus’ in response to ‘Operation Tiger’ allegedly carried out by the Shiv Sena to lure six of its nine Lok Sabha MPs into its fold and refuted allegations his party was likely to merge with the Congress

title: When Chennai’s Veeranam drinking water project hogged the headlines for all the wrong reasons
content: Decades ago, the Veeranam project, meant for bringing water to Chennai, had continued to evoke controversy for long

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Assam government monitoring flash flood situation in Arunachal
content: In view of the heavy rainfall in the upper catchments, a substantial rise in water levels and flow velocity is anticipated in the Brahmaputra river and its tributaries in the downstream areas of Assam

title: Railways, Cochin Port launch inquiries into ‘dismantling’ of defunct rail tracks at Willingdon Island
content: The inquiries started after the track between Q9 Berth of the port and the office of Tata Global Beverages were found dismantled, triggering suspicions about loss of valuable steel rails

title: Behind ‘Son of Thanjai’, the Tamil game that promises to revolutionise Indian gaming
content: Writer-lyricist Madhan Karky and Ayelet Studio head Abraham K take us through the making of their historical open-world game and speak about the challenges of making a rooted Tamil game with international appeal

title: Shivamogga court receives bomb threat
content: Principal District and Sessions Judge G.A. Manjunatha said that he had reported the threat to the police without delay

title: Indian aircraft briefly drifts into Pak airspace, probe ordered
content: the flight reportedly strayed into Pakistani airspace on Tuesday night . instead of landing at Amritsar’s Sri Guru Ramdas Ji International Airport, the flight was diverted back to Delhi due to heavy air traffic . it later departed again from Delhi and landed in Amritar at 2.10am on June 24 .
